message:
fix: update stale root_pid/age on pane re-entry in lifecycle engine

PaneLifecycleEngine::get_or_create_state() previously returned the
existing PaneState without updating its root_pid or age fields when
the pane was already tracked. This meant that if a pane's shell
process was replaced (e.g., after a restart or re-exec), the engine
would continue using the original PID and age, leading to incorrect
health assessments and process triage decisions.

Changes:
  - get_or_create_state() now writes the caller-supplied root_pid
    and age into the existing PaneState on the Some(idx) path,
    ensuring fields stay current across update_pane() calls.
  - Add pane_root_pid(pane_id) public accessor (#[must_use]) so
    callers can inspect the tracked root PID without reaching into
    internals. Mirrors the existing pane_health() accessor pattern.
  - Move TriageAction and TriageCategory imports from module scope
    to #[cfg(test)] since they are only used in test assertions,
    not in production code paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
